model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Iowa State is one of the elite teams in the tournament, ranked 7th in the AP Poll with the 5th-best SRS in the nation (27.15) and the 6th-ranked defense nationally (DRtg 95.6), while Tennessee State's SRS of -4.54 (216th) and schedule strength of 342nd out of 365 teams reveal a 23-9 record built against one of the weakest schedules in Division I. The efficiency gap is enormous—Iowa State's offensive rating of 120.0 (21st) and defensive rating of 95.6 (6th) vastly outclass Tennessee State's 111.5 and 101.5 respectively, and the Cyclones' Big 12-tested roster under T.J. Otzelberger should overwhelm the Tigers in their first NCAA Tournament appearance since 1994. While Tennessee State's respectable defensive rating (56th) gives them one area of competence, it was earned against OVC competition and will not translate against Iowa State's caliber of offense, making this one of the more lopsided 2/15 matchups in the bracket.

reasoningStep.title: Iowa State vs Tennessee State – 2/15 Seed Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Iowa State is a dominant 2-seed with elite metrics across the board (SRS 5th, DRtg 6th, ORtg 21st) facing a 15-seed Tennessee State team whose 23-9 record was built against the 342nd-ranked schedule in the country. The quality gap is massive and consistent with the ~94% historical win rate for 2-seeds over 15-seeds.
evidence: Iowa State: 27-7, AP #7, SRS 27.15 (5th), ORtg 120.0 (21st), DRtg 95.6 (6th), Big 12 3rd place
Tennessee State: 23-9, SRS -4.54 (216th), SOS -8.68 (342nd), ORtg 111.5 (136th), DRtg 101.5 (56th), OVC champions
SRS gap of ~31.7 points is one of the largest in the bracket, reflecting Iowa State's vastly superior competition level
Tennessee State's first NCAA Tournament since 1994 under first-year coach Nolan Smith; 2-seeds historically beat 15-seeds ~94% of the time

The top seeds are elite programs with the potential to cut down the nets. The underdogs are dangerous, too. The field also has undeniable star power. Top transfers such as Yaxel Lendeborg at Michigan and freshman stars (see: AJ Dybantsa , a candidate for the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft) are all in the same tier of teams this year, chasing the same dreams. It's a lot to consider. But we're here to help. Our annual NCAA tournament guide gives you the information you need to know about each team, pertinent information about any current issues that could impact their postseason, key injuries to note -- and, finally, our projections on their ceiling. The top seeds are elite programs with the potential to cut down the nets. The underdogs are dangerous, too. The field also has undeniable star power. Top transfers such as Yaxel Lendeborg at Michigan and freshman stars (see: AJ Dybantsa , a candidate for the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft) are all in the same tier of teams this year, chasing the same dreams. It's a lot to consider. But we're here to help. Our annual NCAA tournament guide gives you the information you need to know about each team, pertinent information about any current issues that could impact their postseason, key injuries to note -- and, finally, our projections on their ceiling. **Iowa State (2-seed):**
- Record: 27-7 (12-6 Big 12, 3rd)
- AP Rank: 7th
- SRS: 27.15 (5th nationally)
- SOS: 10.50 (42nd)
- ORtg: 120.0 (21st), DRtg: 95.6 (6th)
- Tournament ceiling described as "National championship" by ESPN
- Note: The Keshon Gilbert injury article appears to be from a prior year (2025), not 2026

**Tennessee State (15-seed):**
- Record: 23-9 (15-5 OVC, 1st)
- OVC Regular Season & Tournament Champions
- First NCAA Tournament since 1994
- SRS: -4.54 (216th)
- SOS: -8.68 (342nd - one of the weakest schedules)
- ORtg: 111.5 (136th), DRtg: 101.5 (56th)
- First-year coach Nolan Smith

The gap is enormous. Iowa State has the 5th-best SRS in the country and the 6th-best defense, while Tennessee State's SRS of -4.54 and schedule strength of 342nd indicate a team that built its record against extremely weak competition.
